description: Trusting internal balances as final without reconciling against the processor or bank, so silent breaks accumulate undetected.
body: Failure mode: internal records are treated as authoritative and never compared against the external settlement source, so missed fees, failed settlements, or dropped entries accumulate until a large unexplained gap surfaces. Detection signals: no scheduled reconciliation; internal totals diverge from bank/processor reports; breaks discovered only during an audit. Do not assume internal state matches reality without verification. Safer fallback: reconcile against the external source of truth on a schedule, classify every break, and investigate before adjusting. Risk boundary: advisory only; escalate unexplained reconciliation breaks to a human and never post a forcing adjustment to make totals match.
